title: Data-Driven Management
text:
Welcome. This site accompanies the open textbook Data-Driven Management: Statistics for Managers. Here you will find chapter materials, datasets, worked examples, lecture slides, and instructor resources — all free under CC BY 4.0.


Quick links



Resource
Description




Textbook
27 chapters across 8 parts, HTML format


Lecture Slides
RevealJS decks for every unit


Worked Examples
Fully worked R examples with kable output


Datasets
Clean CSV files for all units


For Instructors
Pacing guides, discussion questions, Moodle quizzes


Downloads
All resources in one place






About this resource
This textbook was designed for management students with no prior background in statistics. It emphasises interpretation and decision-making rather than mathematical derivation, and uses realistic business datasets throughout. All R code is shown and reproducible.

title: For Instructors
text:
Instructor packs for all units are available in the password-protected instructor section.
Open instructor resources
Each unit pack includes:

A 75-minute pacing guide with activity timings
Three discussion questions at whole-class, small-group, and challenge levels
Two common misconception notes with corrective framing
Dataset notes and a live data preview
Moodle quiz bundle (XML) aligned to unit objectives



Quiz bundles are in shared/quizzes/moodle_xml/quiz01.xml through quiz05.xml. Import directly via Moodle’s Question Bank import tool using the Moodle XML format.



All materials are released under CC BY 4.0. You may adapt, redistribute, and use them in commercial and non-commercial settings with attribution.

title: Lecture Slides
text:
Each unit has a corresponding RevealJS slide deck for classroom or self-study use. Slides are available as interactive HTML presentations.

Navigate slides with arrow keys. Press F for full screen, S for speaker notes, ? for all keyboard shortcuts.

title: Videos
text: This section is reserved for chapter walkthroughs, worked-solution videos, and short revision clips aligned to the book and slide deck.

title: Worked Examples
text:
Each unit includes a fully worked example using the unit’s dataset. Steps cover data inspection, descriptive summaries, visualisation, and interpretation.
Browse all worked examples



Unit
Example
Dataset




01
Describing branch performance data
ch01_main.csv


02
Descriptive vs inferential reasoning
ch02_main.csv


03
Populations, samples, and sampling error
ch03_main.csv


04
Choosing and evaluating sampling methods
ch04_main.csv


05
Identifying and handling variable types
ch05_main.csv



All examples include R code, kable-formatted output tables, and reflection questions.

title: Textbook
text:
The full textbook is available as a searchable HTML book. It covers all 27 units across eight parts, from descriptive statistics through to choosing and interpreting hypothesis tests.
Open the textbook










Part
Units
Topics




1
01–05
Foundations: what statistics is, populations, sampling, variable types


2
06–10
Describing data: distributions, graphs, measures of centre and spread


3
11–14
Probability: rules, distributions, expected value


4
15–16
Relationships: correlation and regression


5
17–19
Estimation: CLT, confidence intervals


6
20–21
Hypothesis testing logic


7
22–26
Core tests: t-tests, ANOVA, chi-square, non-parametric tests


8
27
Interpretation and decision use



All chapters include worked examples, key term tables, and exercises.

title: Datasets
text:
All datasets are available as clean CSV files in the shared/datasets/dmm/clean/ directory of the project repository.










File
Unit
Description
Rows
Key variables




ch01_main.csv
01
Retail branch performance
47
region, revenue, complaints, rating


ch01_practice.csv
01
Practice subset
30
As above


ch02_main.csv
02
Consultancy analyst performance
120
team, billable hours, satisfaction


ch03_main.csv
03
Retail transactions
200
transaction value, items, channel


ch04_main.csv
04
Employee workforce
600
department, tenure, engagement


ch05_main.csv
05
Hotel guest records
300
room type, spend, satisfaction, loyalty



Practice versions (smaller subsets) are available for each unit. All datasets are synthetic and generated with fixed random seeds for reproducibility.


# From within the project
df <- dmm_load("01", "main")   # uses dmm_helpers.R

# Direct path
df <- read.csv("shared/datasets/dmm/clean/ch01_main.csv")
